input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a complete example:

**XAML (`ItemsPage.xaml`)**

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             xmlns:vm="clr-namespace:MyApp.ViewModels"
             x:Class="MyApp.Views.ItemsPage"
             x:DataType="vm:ItemsViewModel">

    <RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
                 IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">

        <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                        SelectionMode="Single"
                        SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}"
                        SelectionChangedCommand="{Binding ItemSelectedCommand}">
            <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
                <DataTemplate x:DataType="vm:ItemModel">
                    <Grid Padding="10" ColumnDefinitions="*,Auto">
                        <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="16" />
                        <Label Grid.Column="1" Text="{Binding Status}"
                               TextColor="Gray" />
                    </Grid>
                </DataTemplate>
            </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>

            <CollectionView.EmptyView>
                <Label Text="No items found."
                       HorizontalOptions="Center"
                       VerticalOptions="Center" />
            </CollectionView.EmptyView>
        </CollectionView>
    </RefreshView>
</ContentPage>
```

**ViewModel (`ItemsViewModel.cs`)**

```csharp
public partial class ItemsViewModel : ObservableObject
{
    private readonly IApiService _api;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private bool _isRefreshing;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private ItemModel? _selectedItem;

    public ObservableCollection<ItemModel> Items { get; } = new();

    public ItemsViewModel(IApiService api) => _api = api;

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task Refresh()
    {
        try
        {
            var data = await _api.GetItemsAsync();
            Items.Clear();
            foreach (var item in data)
                Items.Add(item);
        }
        finally
        {
            IsRefreshing = false;   // stops the spinner
        }
    }

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task ItemSelected()
    {
        if (SelectedItem is null) return;

        await Shell.Current.GoToAsync(
            $"{nameof(DetailPage)}?id={SelectedItem.Id}");

        SelectedItem = null;        // clear so the same row can be re-tapped
    }
}
```

### Key binding relationships

| XAML Property | ViewModel Member | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `RefreshView.Command` | `RefreshCommand` | Triggers API reload on pull-down |
| `RefreshView.IsRefreshing` | `IsRefreshing` (two-way) | Controls spinner; set `false` when done |
| `CollectionView.ItemsSource` | `ObservableCollection<T>` | Auto-updates UI on add/remove |
| `CollectionView.SelectedItem` | `SelectedItem` (two-way) | Tracks which item the user tapped |
| `SelectionChangedCommand` | `ItemSelectedCommand` | Fires navigation, then nulls selection |

### Things to watch for

- **Set `IsRefreshing = false`** in a `finally` block — if you forget, the spinner never stops.
- **Null out `SelectedItem`** after navigation so the same row can be selected again.
- **`x:DataType`** on both the page and the `DataTemplate` enables compiled bindings (faster, catches typos at build time).
- Uses **CommunityToolkit.Mvvm** source generators (`[ObservableProperty]`, `[RelayCommand]`) to eliminate boilerplate.
